evidence_quotes.quote: threatening websites by stopping the provision of their payment services if sellers do not agree to their terms and conditions.
the same sellers have also had to remove games of worship that do not even contain sexual content.
Those credit companies have forced Steam and Itch.io, which sell video games, to stop selling certain games containing sexual material.

paragraphs.text: My feedback relates to forced censorship and morality currently run by credit companies such as Visa, Mastercard and PayPal. Those credit companies have forced Steam and Itch.io, which sell video games, to stop selling certain games containing sexual material, threatening websites by stopping the provision of their payment services if sellers do not agree to their terms and conditions. This coercion is based on the demand of Collective Shout, an Australian activist movement, that credit companies force video game sellers to terms based on their own morality. Sexual content in games is an easy target that activists try to see as an age-old as a Trojan horse in order to control what others can and must not do. For the same reasons, in addition to games containing sexual content, the same sellers have also had to remove games of worship that do not even contain sexual content. This is, in my view, a worrying, slippery fall that will only worsen and will not help anyone. Since the deleted video games in question do not infringe the law and are entirely lawful, I do not see how it is permissible, in the name of the law, for people who are only mentally inspiring and weaning them to coerce others to their own will and thus to determine where adults may spend their own money. I am particularly worried by the fact that the easy target, this time a game for adults, opens the door only and shows how unrestricted the credit companies have, as they can in practice abuse their power created through the monopoly in order to limit anything they want. This must have something in law, as no profit-making, service-providing international company should have such intrusive and unrestricted power to create its own law in practice and forcing others to live accordingly, at the risk of otherwise denying a mandatory service, which is a digital payment service, in modern life. Imagine, for example, if water or electricity companies can do the same and threaten to cease their services to those with whom they disagree and who otherwise do not agree to censor themselves or the material they create. A long writing apologises, but I will not really be in a position to express my real concern about this situation. In my view, this has revealed a huge weakness in the law and has shown how the current law cannot touch upon the monopoly created by the cartel. Finally, I want to say that I am not in any way interested in activism or in any other way, and I would not like anything more than I can only live independently from such patterns. But this has been for the first time in my life a situation that has led me to only be forced to express my concerns, as this is what, as such, will only and only be worse, and will limit the lives of both myself and others. Thank you in particular if you have read this.
